title: Aphelia drummondii
taxonomy: Aphelia drummondii is classified as a genus of flowering plants in the family Anacampserotaceae. This genus is part of the order Caryophyllales and the class Magnoliopsida. Aphelia drummondii is native to South Africa, where it is found in the Western Cape province. The classification of this plant is based on its morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics. It is a succulent plant, which is a type of plant adapted to store water in its leaves or stems.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease.
